trigger: Metrics data-driven optimization — CC overestimation analysis reveals premature compaction

changes.name: autoCompactWindow 140K→155K
changes.why: CC uses Anthropic tokenizer which over-estimates tokens for Chinese+code+JSON content by ~1.7x. At autoCompactWindow=140K, real tokens at compact trigger = median 82,808 = only 40.8% of ModelScope 202,745 capacity. 135 compacts/day = too frequent. Each auto-compact uses stripNonEssential=true → poor summary quality → CC forgets what it was doing. With 155K: real at trigger ≈ 91K = 45% capacity, fewer compacts, better IQ preservation.
changes.expected_impact: Fewer auto-compacts per session (from ~135/day to ~100/day), better context preservation, less auto-compact quality degradation

key_differences.auto_vs_manual_stripNonEssential: auto=true (truncates tool output, tools=[]), manual=false (full context + all tools)
key_differences.auto_customInstructions: null (no user guidance), manual can have user-provided focus instructions
key_differences.auto_continuation_prompt: forced 'Resume directly — do not acknowledge' → model pretends compact didn't happen
key_differences.auto_thresholdSource: settings → reactive path (NR8, may use background pre-computed summary), manual uses yK$ (full compact)

auto_compact_behavior_analysis.reactive_path_routing: When autoCompactWindow is an explicit number (not 'auto'), CC routes auto-compact through NR8 reactive path which may pre-compute summary in background at earlier time point, missing last few turns of context
